Court and the aforesaid guidelines issued by National Disaster Management
Authority (NDMA), the Government of India has decided to revise the item 1 of
Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) letter No. 32/7/2014-NDM-I, dated 08.04.2015 in
respect of the heading "Gratuitous Relief" by adding a new entry as 1 (f) after 1 (e)
in the 'revised list of items and norms of assistance" under State Disaster Response
(SDRF), as stated below:

No.

per

Text: 50  per  cent  of  seating  capacity  by  following  Standard
Operating Procedure. cinema Theatres' owners should ensure
that all employees are vaccinated.

3
Public Transport to Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka states
shall be permitted by following Standard Operating
Procedure.
k) Creches are permitted to function by following Standard
Operating Procedure. The Creche Management/ Owners should
ensure that all the staff of Creches are vaccinated.
) Swimming pools are allowed to operate with 50 per cent of
trainees for sports training only. Trainers and trainees above 18
years of age should be vaccinated.
m) Tamil Nadu Skill Development Corporation and Tamil Nadu Women
Development Corporation are permitted to conduct employment
training classes by following Standard Operating Procedure.
n) Bars in Hotels and recreational clubs (FL 2 & FL 3) are permitted to
operate.
General1
All EStablishments/organizations/Industries which are permitted to
function should ensure that their employees are vaccinated.
) All shops and places prone for public gathering should follow the
following important Standard Operating Procedures.
Shops shall have mandatory hand hygiene
(hand sanitizer with dispenser) at the entrance and should take
necessary action to install automated thermal screeninng.
Management shall ensure that the workers and customers
wear face masks compulsorily.
All shops and establishments shall ensure that the doors and
>
windows are kept open for cross ventilation. In order to ensure
social distancing norms, more number of persons shall not be
allowed at a time inside the shops.
Specific markings with sufficient distance may be made at the
entrance of the shops to manage the general public standing in
the queue.
Action will taken against the commercial/other organisations
which violate the above guldelines and prescribed limit of
customers.
/P.T.O//

i) Test-Track-Treat-Vaccination-Covid-19 Approprlate Behaviour principle
shall be strictly adhered to.
ii) In order to contain the spread of Covid-19, the Containment Zones
shall be demarcated upto Micro Level and the containment measures
including vaccination to the people in this area shall be initiated by
the District Collectors and the Local Bodies as per the Standard
Operating Procedures.
iv) Within the demarcated Containment Zones, containment
measures shall be scrupulously followed, as under:
Only essential activities shall be allowed in the Containment
Zones. No activity, except medical emergencies and supply of
essential goods and services shall be permitted.
There shall be intensive house-to-house surveillance by
surveillance teams formed for the purpose.
3) The District Collectors and Local Administrations concerned shal
make special efforts to vaccinate all and organise IEC activities by creating
awareness and self-responsibility among the public, in order to contain the
spread of the disease.
4) The public are also requested to seek medical advice of the nearest
hospital as soon as symptoms of infection appear.
5) The public are requested to make use of these relaxations
with responsibility and to make proper use of the decisions taken in
view of the fact that, the livelihood of all people and the education
and future of students should not be affected.
6) The Public are advised to take vaccination and.t o give full
co-operation to the Government's prevention efforts in order to eliminate
spread of Corona infection is in our state.
7) The Government also order that n0 activities shall be permitted in
the Containment Zones.
8) The Commissioner, Greater Chennai Corporation/ District Collectors
and Commissioners of Police/ Superintendents of Police concerned shall take
all necessary measures for the strict enforcement of National Directives for
Covid-19 management viz., wearing of face masks, hand hygiene and
social distancing, screening and hygiene, frequent sanitization of
entire workplace etc., and adherence of the Standard Operating
Procedures issued for the permitted activities. The Commissioner, Greater
Chennai Corporation/ District Collectors concerned may initiate penal action
for imposing fines on persons violating the National Directives.

5
9) Any person violating these measures wll be liable to be proceeded
against as per the provislons of Section 51 to 60 of the Disaster
Management Act, 2005, besides legal action under Section 188 of the IPC,
and other legal provislons as applicable.

Source #1:
Act: Rev E 77 2015
Section: 7 - The  Government  also  direct
Text: 7.  The  Government  also  direct

Identification
Implementation  Committee  (UIDIC)  will  be  eligible  for  the  facilities  usually
allowed  for  such  State  Level  Implementation  Committee  in  the  State,  including
Travelling  Allowance  and  Daily  Allowance  at  the  rates  eligible  for  such  persons
and  no  separate  orders  will  be  issued  in  this  regard.    The  Administration  and
miscellaneous expenditure of the Unique Identification Implementation Committee
(UIDIC)  will  be  debited  to  the  respective  head  of  account  of  the  Administrative
departments to which the above officers belong.

India  has  prescribed  the  following  methods  for  the  release  of  Aadhaar  linked
incentivisation grants by Ministry of Finance:-

i)  The  total  Aadhaar  generated  and  the  Below  Poverty  Line  population
covered  by  the  States  for  Aadhaar  generation  would  be  placed  by  the
States  before  Unique  Identification  Implementation  Committee  (UIDIC)
for recommendation to Unique Identification Authority of India for release
of Aadhaar linked incentivisation grant.

        ii)  Unique  Identification  Authority  of  India  Headquarters  would  check  with
Central  ID  Data  Repository  (CIDR)  data  as  to  the  number  of  Aadhaar
issued  for  the  State  so  that  the  Below  Poverty  Line  beneficiaries  for
whom  the  grant  sought  is  within  the  Aadhaar  number  issued  for  the
State.  The  data  received  from  the  State  would  be  forwarded  by  Unique
Identification  Authority  of  India  to  the  Ministry  of  Finance  for  release  of
Aadhaar linked incentivisation grant.

the bereaved families/persons who met with accidents causing death from
Rs.15,000/-  to  50,000/-  and  also  annual  family  income    ceiling  limit  from
Rs.24,000/-  to  Rs.48,000/-  under  Chief  Minister's  Public  Relief  Fund
(CMPRF) - Orders issued.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Revenue [DM-I(1)] Department

G.O.(Ms) No.89

                                  Dated:18.02.2015

                          Read:

Section: 3 - In the letter second read above, the Principal Secretary/Commissioner of
Text: 3. In the letter second read above, the Principal Secretary/Commissioner of
Revenue  Administration  has  stated  that  the  Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster
Management Authority and Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management Agency have
the  same  acronym  viz.,  TNSDMA,  which  is  used  interchangeably,  causing  much
confusion  even  in  the  usual  course  of  daily  administration.    For  instance,  the
office of the  Accountant General during its performance Audit on Chennai Floods
2015, on many occasions misunderstood “Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management

/p.t.o/

 2

Agency”  as  “Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster  Management  Authority”  due  to  the
acronym  of  Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster  Management  Agency.    Hence,  for  clarity,
he has suggested that the Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management Agency which
is functioning  under the supervision of Commissioner of Revenue Administration,
Disaster  Management  and  Mitigation  Department  may  be      renamed  as  Tamil
Nadu Disaster Risk Reduction Agency, with an acronym of TNDRRA and therefore
requested  orders  of  Government  to  rename  Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster
Management  Agency  (TNSDMA)  as  Tamil  Nadu  Disaster  Risk  Reduction

Section: 4 - The  Additional  Chief  Secretary/  Commissioner  of  Revenue
Text: 4.  The  Additional  Chief  Secretary/  Commissioner  of  Revenue
Administration in his letter fourth read above has stated that at the State level, the
Unique  Identification  Implementation  Committee  chaired  by  the  Chief  Secretary
shall review the utilization of Aadhaar linked incentivisation grants. Hence, he has
suggested  that  the  State  Level  Advisory  Board  constituted  in  the  Government
Order  second  read  above
to  Unique
Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) may be reconstituted. He has also stated
that  it  was  earlier  proposed  that  the  States  would  be  headed  by  Unique
Identification Authority Commissioners in the rank of Joint Secretary, Government
of  India,  under  Central  Staffing  Schemes  and  hence  Unique  Identification

for  coordinating  activities  relating

/p.t.o/

 Authority  Commissioner  was  nominated  as  Member  Secretary.  Since  no  such
post  was  created  in  Tamil  Nadu,  it  is  proposed  that  the  Revenue  Secretary,
Government of Tamil Nadu may be nominated as member secretary. In order to
ensure  the  participation  of  Unique  Identification  Authority  of  India  at  state  level,
one  representative  of  Unique  Identification  Authority  of  India  may  also  be
nominated as a Member.

Order  second  read  above
to  Unique

 Authority  Commissioner  was  nominated  as  Member  Secretary.  Since  no  such

Text: 50  per  cent  of  seating  capacity  by  following  Standard
Operating Procedure. cinema Theatres' owners should ensure

3
Public Transport to Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka states
shall be permitted by following Standard Operating
Procedure.
k) Creches are permitted to function by following Standard
Operating Procedure. The Creche Management/ Owners should
ensure that all the staff of Creches are vaccinated.
) Swimming pools are allowed to operate with 50 per cent of
trainees for sports training only. Trainers and trainees above 18
years of age should be vaccinated.
m) Tamil Nadu Skill Development Corporation and Tamil Nadu Women
Development Corporation are permitted to conduct employment
training classes by following Standard Operating Procedure.
n) Bars in Hotels and recreational clubs (FL 2 & FL 3) are permitted to
operate.
General1
All EStablishments/organizations/Industries which are permitted to
function should ensure that their employees are vaccinated.
) All shops and places prone for public gathering should follow the
following important Standard Operating Procedures.
Shops shall have mandatory hand hygiene
(hand sanitizer with dispenser) at the entrance and should take
necessary action to install automated thermal screeninng.
Management shall ensure that the workers and customers
wear face masks compulsorily.
All shops and establishments shall ensure that the doors and
>
windows are kept open for cross ventilation. In order to ensure
social distancing norms, more number of persons shall not be
allowed at a time inside the shops.
Specific markings with sufficient distance may be made at the
entrance of the shops to manage the general public standing in
the queue.
Action will taken against the commercial/other organisations
which violate the above guldelines and prescribed limit of
customers.
/P.T.O//

i) Test-Track-Treat-Vaccination-Covid-19 Approprlate Behaviour principle
shall be strictly adhered to.
ii) In order to contain the spread of Covid-19, the Containment Zones
shall be demarcated upto Micro Level and the containment measures
including vaccination to the people in this area shall be initiated by
the District Collectors and the Local Bodies as per the Standard
Operating Procedures.
iv) Within the demarcated Containment Zones, containment
measures shall be scrupulously followed, as under:
Only essential activities shall be allowed in the Containment
Zones. No activity, except medical emergencies and supply of
essential goods and services shall be permitted.
There shall be intensive house-to-house surveillance by
surveillance teams formed for the purpose.
3) The District Collectors and Local Administrations concerned shal
make special efforts to vaccinate all and organise IEC activities by creating
awareness and self-responsibility among the public, in order to contain the
spread of the disease.
4) The public are also requested to seek medical advice of the nearest
hospital as soon as symptoms of infection appear.
5) The public are requested to make use of these relaxations
with responsibility and to make proper use of the decisions taken in
view of the fact that, the livelihood of all people and the education
and future of students should not be affected.
6) The Public are advised to take vaccination and.t o give full
co-operation to the Government's prevention efforts in order to eliminate
spread of Corona infection is in our state.
7) The Government also order that n0 activities shall be permitted in
the Containment Zones.
8) The Commissioner, Greater Chennai Corporation/ District Collectors
and Commissioners of Police/ Superintendents of Police concerned shall take
all necessary measures for the strict enforcement of National Directives for
Covid-19 management viz., wearing of face masks, hand hygiene and
social distancing, screening and hygiene, frequent sanitization of
entire workplace etc., and adherence of the Standard Operating
Procedures issued for the permitted activities. The Commissioner, Greater
Chennai Corporation/ District Collectors concerned may initiate penal action
for imposing fines on persons violating the National Directives.

5
9) Any person violating these measures wll be liable to be proceeded
against as per the provislons of Section 51 to 60 of the Disaster
Management Act, 2005, besides legal action under Section 188 of the IPC,
and other legal provislons as applicable.

guidelines for ex-gratia assistance on account of loss of life to the family members of
the persons who died due to COVID-19, as mandated under Section 12(i) of Disaster
Management Act (DMA),  2005 for the minimum standards of relief to  be provided to
the persons affected by disaster.

Affairs (DM Division) have issued Guldelines for ex-gratia to next of the kin of
deceased by COVID-19 under the sectlon 12 (l) of the Disaster Management Act,
2005, and the guidelines for officlal document for COVID-19 death also issued by
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.

context_str:
Source #1:
Act: Revenue E 831 2021
Section: 6 - In the letter sixth read above,, the Additional Chief Secretary/Commissioner
Text: 6. In the letter sixth read above,, the Additional Chief Secretary/Commissioner
of Revenue Administration has stated that, in pursuance of the Hon'ble Supreme
Court Judgement dated 30.06.2021, in Writ Petition (Civil) No.554/2021 and
W.P.(C) No.539/2021, National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) under
Section 12 (ii) of the Disaster Management Act, 2005, has issued guidelines for
ex-gratia assistance to next of kin of the deceased by COVID-19 on 11.09.2021. It
has been further stated that, in pursuance to the direction of the Hon'ble Supreme
Court and the aforesaid guidelines issued by National Disaster Management
Authority (NDMA), the Government of India has decided to revise the item 1 of
Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) letter No. 32/7/2014-NDM-I, dated 08.04.2015 in
respect of the heading "Gratuitous Relief" by adding a new entry as 1 (f) after 1 (e)
in the 'revised list of items and norms of assistance" under State Disaster Response
(SDRF), as stated below:
SI.
Items Norm
No.
| 1. Ex-Gratia Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty thousand only)
per
payment to deceased person including those involved in relief
next of kin operations or associated in preparedness activities,
of the subject to the cause of death being certified as
deceased COVID-19 as per guidelines jointly issued by
due to Ministry of Health and Family Welfare and Indian
COVID-19. Council of Medical Research on 3dS eptember 2021.
Expenditure on this item will be incurred
from SDRF only, in strict compliance with the
aforesaid NDMA guidelines dated 11th
September, 2021.
This ex-gratia assistance will be applicable
from the date of first COVID-19 case
reported in the country and will continue till
de-notification of COVID-19 as a disaster or
till further orders, whicheveris earlier.

3

Section: 7 - The Aditional Chief Secretary/ Commissioner of Revenue Administration
Text: 7.  The Aditional Chief Secretary/ Commissioner of Revenue Administration
has  requested  the  Government  to  issue  amendment to  the  G.O.(Ms)  No.380,
Revenue and Disaster Management Department, dated: 27.10.2015 by incorporating
the above details as 1 () after 1 (e) under the item  1 "Gratuitous Relief" and also
requested the Government to empower the District Collectors to  invoke the clause

COVID-19.

Source #2:
Act: Revenue E 234 2018
Section: 2 - The Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management Agency (TNSDMA) has its
Text: 2.  The Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management Agency (TNSDMA) has its
own  bye  laws  and  is  administered  by  a  Governing  Council  and  an  Executive
Council.  The  Hon’ble  Minister  for  Revenue  and  Disaster  Management  is  the
Chairperson  of  the  Governing  Council  with  Chief  Secretary  and  Secretary,
Revenue,  are  the  Vice  Chairpersons.  The  Executive  Council  of  the  Agency  is
headed  by  the  Principal  Secretary/Commissioner  of  Revenue  Administration.
Director/Commissioner,  Disaster  Management  is  the  Chief  Executive  Officer  and
Member Secretary of the Agency.

GO.Ms.No.339, Revenue and Disaster Management (DM-IV) Department,
dated 31.03.2021 with various relaxations and certain restrictions.
query: Provide the official text of Section 7 under the Revenue E 831 2021.
output:
7.  The Aditional Chief Secretary/ Commissioner of Revenue Administration
has  requested  the  Government  to  issue  amendment to  the  G.O.(Ms)  No.380,
Revenue and Disaster Management Department, dated: 27.10.2015 by incorporating
the above details as 1 () after 1 (e) under the item  1 "Gratuitous Relief" and also
requested the Government to empower the District Collectors to  invoke the clause
TR  27  for  drawal  of funds  in  order  to  provide  immediate  relief to  the  victims  of
COVID-19.

Section: 6 - The  Government  have  examined  the  proposal  of  the  Additional
Text: 6.    The  Government  have  examined  the  proposal  of  the  Additional
Chief  Secretary/  Commissioner  of  Revenue  Administration  and  decided  to
enhance  the  financial  assistance  as  relief  amount  under  Chief  Minister's
Public  Relief  Fund  and  accordingly  direct  that  the  financial  assistance

enhanced  from  Rs.15,000/-  to  Rs.50,000/-    (Rupees  Fifty  thousand  only)
per head for the victims in the event of death and the annual family income
of the victim is also  revised from Rs.24,000/- to Rs.48,000/- (Rupees Forty
eight thousand only) under Chief Minister's Public Relief Fund.

Text: 5.    The  Principal  Secretary  /  Commissioner  of  Land  Reforms  has  also  stated
that the Chennai Metropolitan Development Authority has requested to handover the
above  lands,  as  it  is  essentially  required  for  formation  of  Mofussil  Bus  stand  at
Vandalur,  in  order  to  create  major  public  infrastructure  to  decongest  traffic  in
Chennai City and to give enter upon permission pending land alienation and land cost
and that the Chennai Metropolitan Development Authority has also stated that they
are  ready  to  pay  any  advance  amount  towards  land  cost  pending  fixation  of  actual
land cost by the Government.

Source #1:
Act: Rev E 487 2014
Section: 6 - Considering the prevailing flood situation and based on the request of
Text: 6. Considering the prevailing flood situation and based on the request of
the Heads of Departments, the State Relief Commissioner and  Additional Chief
Secretary/Commissioner of Revenue Administration has recommended to sanction
a sum of Rs.60.00 crore under State Disaster Response Fund (SDRF), towards
repair for the damages caused to roads and tanks due to incessant rain and
floods  during  North  East  Monsoon  -2014  to  the  implementing  agencies  by
apportioning it in the following manner:

| Highways Department       |     |     | -   | Rs.30.00 crore       |
| ------------------------- | --- | --- | --- | -------------------- |
| Public Works              |     |     | -   | Rs.10.00 crore       |
| Rural Development         |     |     | -   | Rs.10.00 crore       |
| Municipal Administration  |     |     | -   | Rs.10.00 crore       |
|                           |     |     |     | -------------------  |
| Total                     |     |     | -   | Rs.60.00 crore       |
                                                         --------------------

Act: Rev E 487 2014
Section: 7 - The  Government  have  examined  the  above  proposal  in  detail  and
Text: 7.  The  Government  have  examined  the  above  proposal  in  detail  and
agreed to the recommendation of the State Relief Commissioner and Additional
Chief Secretary/Commissioner of Revenue Administration and accord sanction for
a  sum  of  Rs.60.00  crore  (Rupees  Sixty  crore  only)  under  the  State  Disaster
Response Fund(SDRF) towards repair for the damages caused to roads and tanks
due  to  incessant  rain  and  floods  during  North  East  Monsoon-2014  to  the
implementing agencies as detailed below:-

| Highways Department                    |     |     | -   | Rs.30.00 crore       |

have  decided  to  accept  the  recommendations  of  the  Additional  Chief  Secretary
Commissioner  of  Revenue  Administration  with  some  modifications  and  hereby
issues the guidelines and  procedures in  supercession of the orders issued earlier  in

above  annual  family  income  is  fixed  as  Rs.24,000/-  to  get  relief  under
Distress Relief Scheme. Though the income limit fixed as Rs.24,000/- to
get  relief  under  Distress  Relief  Scheme  exists,  the  same  analogy  is  being
followed  to  get  relief  under  Chief  Minister's  Public  Relief  Fund  also.    Till
date,  this  income  limit  is  in  force  for  more  than  5  years  without  any
revision.  He  has  also  suggested  that  in  view  of  the  price  hike  in  essential
things  at  present,  the  annual  family  income  of  victim  for  eligibility  under
Chief  Minister's  Public  Relief  Fund    may  be  revised  from  Rs.24,000/-  to
Rs.48,000/-

Chief  Minister's  Public  Relief  Fund    may  be  revised  from  Rs.24,000/-  to
Rs.48,000/-

Section: 4 - From  the  Principal  Secretary  /  Commissioner  of  Land  Reforms,  Letter
Text: 4. From  the  Principal  Secretary  /  Commissioner  of  Land  Reforms,  Letter
    No.B1/6315/2012, (L.Ref.), dated 02.09.2016.

ORDER:

*****

In the reference first read above, orders have been issued resuming the lands
measuring to an extent of 88.52 acres which were declared as surplus lands from the
holdings of Tvl. Kasturi Estate Private Limited in Kilambakkam Village, Chengalpattu
Taluk  of  Kancheepuram  District  under  rule  9(1)(iii)(b)  of  the  Tamil  Nadu  Land
Reforms (Disposal of Surplus Land) Rules, 1965 so as to reserve the above lands for
ELCOT, Indian Institute of Technology and Neyveli Lignite Corporation under rule 13
of the said Rules.

Section: 4 - From  the  Principal  Secretary  /  Commissioner  of  Land  Reforms,  Letter
Text: 4. From  the  Principal  Secretary  /  Commissioner  of  Land  Reforms,  Letter
    No.B1/6315/2012, (L.Ref.), dated 02.09.2016.

ORDER:

*****

In the reference first read above, orders have been issued resuming the lands
measuring to an extent of 88.52 acres which were declared as surplus lands from the
holdings of Tvl. Kasturi Estate Private Limited in Kilambakkam Village, Chengalpattu
Taluk  of  Kancheepuram  District  under  rule  9(1)(iii)(b)  of  the  Tamil  Nadu  Land
Reforms (Disposal of Surplus Land) Rules, 1965 so as to reserve the above lands for
ELCOT, Indian Institute of Technology and Neyveli Lignite Corporation under rule 13
of the said Rules.

ORDER:

*****

In the reference first read above, orders have been issued resuming the lands
measuring to an extent of 88.52 acres which were declared as surplus lands from the
holdings of Tvl. Kasturi Estate Private Limited in Kilambakkam Village, Chengalpattu
Taluk  of  Kancheepuram  District  under  rule  9(1)(iii)(b)  of  the  Tamil  Nadu  Land
Reforms (Disposal of Surplus Land) Rules, 1965 so as to reserve the above lands for
ELCOT, Indian Institute of Technology and Neyveli Lignite Corporation under rule 13
of the said Rules.

Act: Rev E 478 2022
Section: 7 - The  following  provisions  for  Appeal/  Revision  is  provided  for the
Text: 7.  The  following  provisions  for  Appeal/  Revision  is  provided  for the

aggrieved  persons

7 (1). Appeal

Against the order of the Tahsildhar,  an  appeal  petition  can  be filed  to
the  respective  Revenue  Divisional  Officer within  a  period  of one year from  the
date of issuance/rejection of the application,  as the case may be.

7(2). Revision

The  power of revision  over the orders  passed  by the  Revenue  Divisional
Officer  lies  with  the  District  Revenue Officer and  Revision  petition  shall  be fled
within  a  period of 1(one) year from  the date of passing of the order in  appeal.

Source #1:
Act: Rev E 478 2022
Section: 7 - The  following  provisions  for  Appeal/  Revision  is  provided  for the
Text: 7.  The  following  provisions  for  Appeal/  Revision  is  provided  for the

aggrieved  persons

7 (1). Appeal

Against the order of the Tahsildhar,  an  appeal  petition  can  be filed  to
the  respective  Revenue  Divisional  Officer within  a  period  of one year from  the
date of issuance/rejection of the application,  as the case may be.

7(2). Revision

The  power of revision  over the orders  passed  by the  Revenue  Divisional
Officer  lies  with  the  District  Revenue Officer and  Revision  petition  shall  be fled
within  a  period of 1(one) year from  the date of passing of the order in  appeal.

Source #2:
Act: Rev E 478 2022
Section: 3 - The  Village  Administrative  Officer  /  Revenue  Inspector  must
Text: 3.  The  Village  Administrative  Officer  /  Revenue  Inspector  must
verify  the applied  documents  and  recommend  with  reasons  for
acceptance/ with  modifications,  if  any,  and  forward  to  the
Revenue  Inspector/Tahsildar  in  one  week.  Revenue  Inspector
has  to  enquire  within  one  week  and  certificate  will  be  issued  by
Tahsildar  within  one  week  after  the  receipt  of  report  from  the
Revenue Inspector.

Section: 6 - In the letter sixth read above,, the Additional Chief Secretary/Commissioner
Text: 6. In the letter sixth read above,, the Additional Chief Secretary/Commissioner
of Revenue Administration has stated that, in pursuance of the Hon'ble Supreme
Court Judgement dated 30.06.2021, in Writ Petition (Civil) No.554/2021 and
W.P.(C) No.539/2021, National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) under
Section 12 (ii) of the Disaster Management Act, 2005, has issued guidelines for
ex-gratia assistance to next of kin of the deceased by COVID-19 on 11.09.2021. It
has been further stated that, in pursuance to the direction of the Hon'ble Supreme
Court and the aforesaid guidelines issued by National Disaster Management
Authority (NDMA), the Government of India has decided to revise the item 1 of
Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) letter No. 32/7/2014-NDM-I, dated 08.04.2015 in
respect of the heading "Gratuitous Relief" by adding a new entry as 1 (f) after 1 (e)
in the 'revised list of items and norms of assistance" under State Disaster Response
(SDRF), as stated below:
SI.
Items Norm
No.
| 1. Ex-Gratia Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty thousand only)
per
payment to deceased person including those involved in relief
next of kin operations or associated in preparedness activities,
of the subject to the cause of death being certified as
deceased COVID-19 as per guidelines jointly issued by
due to Ministry of Health and Family Welfare and Indian
COVID-19. Council of Medical Research on 3dS eptember 2021.
Expenditure on this item will be incurred
from SDRF only, in strict compliance with the
aforesaid NDMA guidelines dated 11th
September, 2021.
This ex-gratia assistance will be applicable
from the date of first COVID-19 case
reported in the country and will continue till
de-notification of COVID-19 as a disaster or
till further orders, whicheveris earlier.

3

Act: Rev E 77 2015
Section: 3 - In  the  letter  third  read  above,  the  Unique  Identification  Authority  of
Text: 3.    In  the  letter  third  read  above,  the  Unique  Identification  Authority  of
India  has  prescribed  the  following  methods  for  the  release  of  Aadhaar  linked
incentivisation grants by Ministry of Finance:-

i)  The  total  Aadhaar  generated  and  the  Below  Poverty  Line  population
covered  by  the  States  for  Aadhaar  generation  would  be  placed  by  the
States  before  Unique  Identification  Implementation  Committee  (UIDIC)
for recommendation to Unique Identification Authority of India for release
of Aadhaar linked incentivisation grant.

        ii)  Unique  Identification  Authority  of  India  Headquarters  would  check  with
Central  ID  Data  Repository  (CIDR)  data  as  to  the  number  of  Aadhaar
issued  for  the  State  so  that  the  Below  Poverty  Line  beneficiaries  for
whom  the  grant  sought  is  within  the  Aadhaar  number  issued  for  the
State.  The  data  received  from  the  State  would  be  forwarded  by  Unique
Identification  Authority  of  India  to  the  Ministry  of  Finance  for  release  of
Aadhaar linked incentivisation grant.

to  Unique

 Authority  Commissioner  was  nominated  as  Member  Secretary.  Since  no  such

Unique  Identification  Implementation  Committee  chaired  by  the  Chief  Secretary
shall review the utilization of Aadhaar linked incentivisation grants. Hence, he has
suggested  that  the  State  Level  Advisory  Board  constituted  in  the  Government
Order  second  read  above
to  Unique
Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) may be reconstituted. He has also stated
that  it  was  earlier  proposed  that  the  States  would  be  headed  by  Unique
Identification Authority Commissioners in the rank of Joint Secretary, Government
of  India,  under  Central  Staffing  Schemes  and  hence  Unique  Identification

for  coordinating  activities  relating

/p.t.o/

 Authority  Commissioner  was  nominated  as  Member  Secretary.  Since  no  such
post  was  created  in  Tamil  Nadu,  it  is  proposed  that  the  Revenue  Secretary,
Government of Tamil Nadu may be nominated as member secretary. In order to
ensure  the  participation  of  Unique  Identification  Authority  of  India  at  state  level,
one  representative  of  Unique  Identification  Authority  of  India  may  also  be
nominated as a Member.

context_str:
Source #1:
Act: Revenue E 234 2018
Section: 3 - In the letter second read above, the Principal Secretary/Commissioner of
Text: 3. In the letter second read above, the Principal Secretary/Commissioner of
Revenue  Administration  has  stated  that  the  Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster
Management Authority and Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management Agency have
the  same  acronym  viz.,  TNSDMA,  which  is  used  interchangeably,  causing  much
confusion  even  in  the  usual  course  of  daily  administration.    For  instance,  the
office of the  Accountant General during its performance Audit on Chennai Floods
2015, on many occasions misunderstood “Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management

/p.t.o/

 2

Agency”  as  “Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster  Management  Authority”  due  to  the
acronym  of  Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster  Management  Agency.    Hence,  for  clarity,
he has suggested that the Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management Agency which
is functioning  under the supervision of Commissioner of Revenue Administration,
Disaster  Management  and  Mitigation  Department  may  be      renamed  as  Tamil
Nadu Disaster Risk Reduction Agency, with an acronym of TNDRRA and therefore
requested  orders  of  Government  to  rename  Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster
Management  Agency  (TNSDMA)  as  Tamil  Nadu  Disaster  Risk  Reduction
Agency (TNDRRA).

Act: Revenue E 234 2018
Section: 2 - From
Text: 2.  From

the  Principal  Secretary/Commissioner  of  Revenue
Administration Letter No.NC.III(2)/3551/2014, dated 11.05.2018.

                                         ******

ORDER:

In  the  Government  Order  first  read  above,  Government  issued  orders  for
the  establishment  of  an  executive  agency  viz.,  Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster
Management  Agency  and  to  register  it  under  the  Tamil  Nadu  Societies
Registration  Act  to  assist  the  Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster  Management  Authority
headed  by  the  Hon’ble  Chief  Minister  as  the  Chairperson  as  per  the  Disaster
Management  Act  2005,  to  monitor  and  review  the  status  of  prevention,
management,  mitigation,  response  and  relief  activities  in  the  State.  The  Tamil
Nadu  State  Disaster  Management  Authority  (TNSDMA)  is  assisted  by  the  State
Executive Committee chaired by the Chief Secretary.

Section: 50 - per  cent  of  seating  capacity  by  following  Standard
Text: 50  per  cent  of  seating  capacity  by  following  Standard
Operating Procedure. cinema Theatres' owners should ensure
that all employees are vaccinated.

3
Public Transport to Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka states
shall be permitted by following Standard Operating
Procedure.
k) Creches are permitted to function by following Standard
Operating Procedure. The Creche Management/ Owners should
ensure that all the staff of Creches are vaccinated.
) Swimming pools are allowed to operate with 50 per cent of
trainees for sports training only. Trainers and trainees above 18
years of age should be vaccinated.
m) Tamil Nadu Skill Development Corporation and Tamil Nadu Women
Development Corporation are permitted to conduct employment
training classes by following Standard Operating Procedure.
n) Bars in Hotels and recreational clubs (FL 2 & FL 3) are permitted to
operate.
General1
All EStablishments/organizations/Industries which are permitted to
function should ensure that their employees are vaccinated.
) All shops and places prone for public gathering should follow the
following important Standard Operating Procedures.
Shops shall have mandatory hand hygiene
(hand sanitizer with dispenser) at the entrance and should take
necessary action to install automated thermal screeninng.
Management shall ensure that the workers and customers
wear face masks compulsorily.
All shops and establishments shall ensure that the doors and
>
windows are kept open for cross ventilation. In order to ensure
social distancing norms, more number of persons shall not be
allowed at a time inside the shops.
Specific markings with sufficient distance may be made at the
entrance of the shops to manage the general public standing in
the queue.
Action will taken against the commercial/other organisations
which violate the above guldelines and prescribed limit of
customers.
/P.T.O//

i) Test-Track-Treat-Vaccination-Covid-19 Approprlate Behaviour principle
shall be strictly adhered to.
ii) In order to contain the spread of Covid-19, the Containment Zones
shall be demarcated upto Micro Level and the containment measures
including vaccination to the people in this area shall be initiated by
the District Collectors and the Local Bodies as per the Standard
Operating Procedures.
iv) Within the demarcated Containment Zones, containment
measures shall be scrupulously followed, as under:
Only essential activities shall be allowed in the Containment
Zones. No activity, except medical emergencies and supply of
essential goods and services shall be permitted.
There shall be intensive house-to-house surveillance by
surveillance teams formed for the purpose.
3) The District Collectors and Local Administrations concerned shal
make special efforts to vaccinate all and organise IEC activities by creating
awareness and self-responsibility among the public, in order to contain the
spread of the disease.
4) The public are also requested to seek medical advice of the nearest
hospital as soon as symptoms of infection appear.
5) The public are requested to make use of these relaxations
with responsibility and to make proper use of the decisions taken in
view of the fact that, the livelihood of all people and the education
and future of students should not be affected.
6) The Public are advised to take vaccination and.t o give full
co-operation to the Government's prevention efforts in order to eliminate
spread of Corona infection is in our state.
7) The Government also order that n0 activities shall be permitted in
the Containment Zones.
8) The Commissioner, Greater Chennai Corporation/ District Collectors
and Commissioners of Police/ Superintendents of Police concerned shall take
all necessary measures for the strict enforcement of National Directives for
Covid-19 management viz., wearing of face masks, hand hygiene and
social distancing, screening and hygiene, frequent sanitization of
entire workplace etc., and adherence of the Standard Operating
Procedures issued for the permitted activities. The Commissioner, Greater
Chennai Corporation/ District Collectors concerned may initiate penal action
for imposing fines on persons violating the National Directives.

5
9) Any person violating these measures wll be liable to be proceeded
against as per the provislons of Section 51 to 60 of the Disaster
Management Act, 2005, besides legal action under Section 188 of the IPC,
and other legal provislons as applicable.
(By Order of the Governor)

) Swimming pools are allowed to operate with 50 per cent of

General1
All EStablishments/organizations/Industries which are permitted to
function should ensure that their employees are vaccinated.
) All shops and places prone for public gathering should follow the
following important Standard Operating Procedures.
Shops shall have mandatory hand hygiene
(hand sanitizer with dispenser) at the entrance and should take
necessary action to install automated thermal screeninng.
Management shall ensure that the workers and customers
wear face masks compulsorily.
All shops and establishments shall ensure that the doors and
>
windows are kept open for cross ventilation. In order to ensure
social distancing norms, more number of persons shall not be
allowed at a time inside the shops.
Specific markings with sufficient distance may be made at the

the queue.
Action will taken against the commercial/other organisations
which violate the above guldelines and prescribed limit of
customers.
/P.T.O//

i) Test-Track-Treat-Vaccination-Covid-19 Approprlate Behaviour principle
shall be strictly adhered to.
ii) In order to contain the spread of Covid-19, the Containment Zones
shall be demarcated upto Micro Level and the containment measures
including vaccination to the people in this area shall be initiated by
the District Collectors and the Local Bodies as per the Standard
Operating Procedures.
iv) Within the demarcated Containment Zones, containment
measures shall be scrupulously followed, as under:
Only essential activities shall be allowed in the Containment
Zones. No activity, except medical emergencies and supply of
essential goods and services shall be permitted.
There shall be intensive house-to-house surveillance by
surveillance teams formed for the purpose.
3) The District Collectors and Local Administrations concerned shal
make special efforts to vaccinate all and organise IEC activities by creating
awareness and self-responsibility among the public, in order to contain the
spread of the disease.
4) The public are also requested to seek medical advice of the nearest
hospital as soon as symptoms of infection appear.
5) The public are requested to make use of these relaxations
with responsibility and to make proper use of the decisions taken in
view of the fact that, the livelihood of all people and the education
and future of students should not be affected.
6) The Public are advised to take vaccination and.t o give full
co-operation to the Government's prevention efforts in order to eliminate
spread of Corona infection is in our state.
7) The Government also order that n0 activities shall be permitted in
the Containment Zones.
8) The Commissioner, Greater Chennai Corporation/ District Collectors
and Commissioners of Police/ Superintendents of Police concerned shall take
all necessary measures for the strict enforcement of National Directives for
Covid-19 management viz., wearing of face masks, hand hygiene and
social distancing, screening and hygiene, frequent sanitization of
entire workplace etc., and adherence of the Standard Operating
Procedures issued for the permitted activities. The Commissioner, Greater
Chennai Corporation/ District Collectors concerned may initiate penal action
for imposing fines on persons violating the National Directives.

9) Any person violating these measures wll be liable to be proceeded
against as per the provislons of Section 51 to 60 of the Disaster
Management Act, 2005, besides legal action under Section 188 of the IPC,
and other legal provislons as applicable.

Text: 2.  The Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management Agency (TNSDMA) has its
own  bye  laws  and  is  administered  by  a  Governing  Council  and  an  Executive
Council.  The  Hon’ble  Minister  for  Revenue  and  Disaster  Management  is  the

Revenue,  are  the  Vice  Chairpersons.  The  Executive  Council  of  the  Agency  is
headed  by  the  Principal  Secretary/Commissioner  of  Revenue  Administration.
Director/Commissioner,  Disaster  Management  is  the  Chief  Executive  Officer  and
Member Secretary of the Agency.

measuring to an extent of 88.52 acres which were declared as surplus lands from the
holdings of Tvl. Kasturi Estate Private Limited in Kilambakkam Village, Chengalpattu
Taluk  of  Kancheepuram  District  under  rule  9(1)(iii)(b)  of  the  Tamil  Nadu  Land

of the said Rules.

Source #3:
Act: Rev E 540 2014
Section: 3 - Orders of High Court of Madras in W.P.No.26722/2013, dated 08.10.2014.
Text: 3.  Orders of High Court of Madras in W.P.No.26722/2013, dated 08.10.2014.

ORDER:

----

In  reference  first  read  above,  the  Hon'ble  High  Court,  in  its  order  dated
11.08.2014 in W.P.No.26722/13 & M.P.No.1/13 filed by Thiru U.A. Marappa Gounder,
has ordered as follows:-

The State Government must set forth a mechanism where aggrieved
persons  can  complain  and  remedial  action  can  be  taken.    Promptly,  they
are  called  upon  to  do  so.    We  have  thus,  requested  the  learned
Government  Pleader  to  enter  appearance  in  this  matter  and  to  obtain
instructions from the State Government, in what manner such an authority
be set up, due  publicity  given to  the  authorities where aggrieved  persons
would  be  first  required  to  make  their  representations  and  a  remedial
action and reasoned response be communicated within a period of 30 days
from such complaint being made.  Once such a mechanism is set up, there
would  be  no  occasion  to  entertain  a  PIL,  on  the  first  instance  in  such
matters as an administrative machinery would be available for remedying
the position.

2.

Based  on  the  directions  of  the  orders  of  the  Hon'ble  High  Court  of
Madras, dated 08.10.2014, the Government have carefully examined the proposal of
the Additional Chief Secretary and Commissioner of Land Administration in reference

//p.t.o.//

 2

second  read  above  and  hereby,  accord  sanction  for  constitution  of  Redressal
Committees for eviction of encroachment with the following procedures:-

(i)

General Procedure: In general, as per Section 7 of Tamil Nadu Land
Encroachment  Act,  1905,  before  taking  proceedings,  the  Collector  or
Tahsildar shall cause to serve on the person a notice calling upon him
to  show  cause  why  he  should  not  be  evicted.    Further  as  per  notice
under Section 6 of the Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905, any
person unauthorizedly occupying any land may be summarily evicted by
the  Collector,  Tahsildar,  Deputy  Tahsildar  (or  any  other  officers
authorized  by  the  State  Government  in  this  regard)  by  serving  such
notices  in  the  manner  prescribed  under  Section  25  of  Tamil  Nadu
Revenue Recovery Act, 1864.

(ii)  Original Petition to be filed at Taluk level:

(cid:153)  The  petitioners  seeking  grievances  for  eviction  of  encroachment  on
Government land may file original petition before the respective Tahsildars.

(cid:153)  On receipt of such petition, the Tahsildar may cause inspection at least at
the  level  of  Deputy  Tahsildar,  to  decide  whether  the  Government  land  is
being  encroached  or  not.    If  convinced  of  the  fact  that  the  Government
land  is  being  encroached,  he  shall  serve  a  "Show  Cause  Notice"  to  the
encroacher under Section 7 of Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905.
Subsequently,  other  procedure  as  laid  down  under  the  Tamil  Nadu  Land
Encroachment Act should be followed within a stipulated time.

(cid:153)  A  detailed  written  reply  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  giving  details  of
the action, if any, taken as per Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905
and the entire process shall be completed within 60 days from the date of
receipt of petition.

(iii)  Appellate Committee at Divisional level:

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  admit  only  the  Appeal  Petitions  on  eviction  of
encroachment  either  on  the  ground  of  failure  on  the  part  of  Tahsildar  to
give reply within 60 days or if the petitioner is not satisfied with the reply
given or action taken by the Tahsildar.

(cid:153)  The Appellate Committee shall be constituted with the following officials:

 1) Revenue Divisional Officer;
 2) Deputy Superintendent of Police; and
 3) Deputy Inspector of Survey.

 3

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  meet  atleast  once  in  a  month  and  decide  each  and
every case individually after giving an opportunity for the petitioner to be
heard.  The Committee shall also take up personal inspection, if required,
along with Tahsildar and Surveyors, after giving advance intimation to the
petitioner.

(cid:153)  A  written  communication  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  conveying  the
decision  of  the  above  Appellate  Committee  within  30  days  from  the
date of receipt of the Appeal Petition.

(iv)  Review Committee at District Level:

(cid:153)  Any  petitioner,  who  does  not  receive  reply  from  the  above  Appellate
Committee  within  the  stipulated  time  or  is  not  satisfied  with  the  decision  of
the  Appellate  Committee,  may  file  Review  Petition  before  the  Review
Committee.

(cid:153)  The  District  Level  Review  Committee  shall  be  constituted  with  the  following

officials:

1) District Revenue Officer;
2) Superintendent of Police; and
3) Assistant Director of Survey.

(cid:153)  The District Level Review Committee shall meet at least once in a month
and communicate its finding, within 30 days from the date of receipt of such
petition, in a similar manner as described para 2 (iii).

(v)  District Level Steering Committee:

(cid:153)  The details and nature of complaints received by the 'Divisional Level Review
Committee'  and  'District  Level  Review  Committee'  along  with  the  gist  of
decisions taken, shall be presented before the existing 'District level Steering
Committee on Encroachment' headed by District Collector already constituted
vide G.O.Ms.No.105 Revenue Department dated 07.03.2001.

The State Government must set forth a mechanism where aggrieved
persons  can  complain  and  remedial  action  can  be  taken.    Promptly,  they
are  called  upon  to  do  so.    We  have  thus,  requested  the  learned
Government  Pleader  to  enter  appearance  in  this  matter  and  to  obtain
instructions from the State Government, in what manner such an authority
be set up, due  publicity  given to  the  authorities where aggrieved  persons
would  be  first  required  to  make  their  representations  and  a  remedial
action and reasoned response be communicated within a period of 30 days
from such complaint being made.  Once such a mechanism is set up, there
would  be  no  occasion  to  entertain  a  PIL,  on  the  first  instance  in  such
matters as an administrative machinery would be available for remedying
the position.

context_str:
Source #1:
Act: Rev E 540 2014
Section: 3 - Orders of High Court of Madras in W.P.No.26722/2013, dated 08.10.2014.
Text: 3.  Orders of High Court of Madras in W.P.No.26722/2013, dated 08.10.2014.

ORDER:

----

In  reference  first  read  above,  the  Hon'ble  High  Court,  in  its  order  dated
11.08.2014 in W.P.No.26722/13 & M.P.No.1/13 filed by Thiru U.A. Marappa Gounder,
has ordered as follows:-

The State Government must set forth a mechanism where aggrieved
persons  can  complain  and  remedial  action  can  be  taken.    Promptly,  they
are  called  upon  to  do  so.    We  have  thus,  requested  the  learned
Government  Pleader  to  enter  appearance  in  this  matter  and  to  obtain
instructions from the State Government, in what manner such an authority
be set up, due  publicity  given to  the  authorities where aggrieved  persons
would  be  first  required  to  make  their  representations  and  a  remedial
action and reasoned response be communicated within a period of 30 days
from such complaint being made.  Once such a mechanism is set up, there
would  be  no  occasion  to  entertain  a  PIL,  on  the  first  instance  in  such
matters as an administrative machinery would be available for remedying
the position.

2.

Based  on  the  directions  of  the  orders  of  the  Hon'ble  High  Court  of
Madras, dated 08.10.2014, the Government have carefully examined the proposal of
the Additional Chief Secretary and Commissioner of Land Administration in reference

//p.t.o.//

 2

second  read  above  and  hereby,  accord  sanction  for  constitution  of  Redressal
Committees for eviction of encroachment with the following procedures:-

(i)

General Procedure: In general, as per Section 7 of Tamil Nadu Land
Encroachment  Act,  1905,  before  taking  proceedings,  the  Collector  or
Tahsildar shall cause to serve on the person a notice calling upon him
to  show  cause  why  he  should  not  be  evicted.    Further  as  per  notice
under Section 6 of the Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905, any
person unauthorizedly occupying any land may be summarily evicted by
the  Collector,  Tahsildar,  Deputy  Tahsildar  (or  any  other  officers
authorized  by  the  State  Government  in  this  regard)  by  serving  such
notices  in  the  manner  prescribed  under  Section  25  of  Tamil  Nadu
Revenue Recovery Act, 1864.

(ii)  Original Petition to be filed at Taluk level:

(cid:153)  The  petitioners  seeking  grievances  for  eviction  of  encroachment  on
Government land may file original petition before the respective Tahsildars.

(cid:153)  On receipt of such petition, the Tahsildar may cause inspection at least at
the  level  of  Deputy  Tahsildar,  to  decide  whether  the  Government  land  is
being  encroached  or  not.    If  convinced  of  the  fact  that  the  Government
land  is  being  encroached,  he  shall  serve  a  "Show  Cause  Notice"  to  the
encroacher under Section 7 of Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905.
Subsequently,  other  procedure  as  laid  down  under  the  Tamil  Nadu  Land
Encroachment Act should be followed within a stipulated time.

(cid:153)  A  detailed  written  reply  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  giving  details  of
the action, if any, taken as per Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905
and the entire process shall be completed within 60 days from the date of
receipt of petition.

(iii)  Appellate Committee at Divisional level:

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  admit  only  the  Appeal  Petitions  on  eviction  of
encroachment  either  on  the  ground  of  failure  on  the  part  of  Tahsildar  to
give reply within 60 days or if the petitioner is not satisfied with the reply
given or action taken by the Tahsildar.

(cid:153)  The Appellate Committee shall be constituted with the following officials:

 1) Revenue Divisional Officer;
 2) Deputy Superintendent of Police; and
 3) Deputy Inspector of Survey.

 3

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  meet  atleast  once  in  a  month  and  decide  each  and
every case individually after giving an opportunity for the petitioner to be
heard.  The Committee shall also take up personal inspection, if required,
along with Tahsildar and Surveyors, after giving advance intimation to the
petitioner.

(cid:153)  A  written  communication  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  conveying  the
decision  of  the  above  Appellate  Committee  within  30  days  from  the
date of receipt of the Appeal Petition.

(iv)  Review Committee at District Level:

(cid:153)  Any  petitioner,  who  does  not  receive  reply  from  the  above  Appellate
Committee  within  the  stipulated  time  or  is  not  satisfied  with  the  decision  of
the  Appellate  Committee,  may  file  Review  Petition  before  the  Review
Committee.

(cid:153)  The  District  Level  Review  Committee  shall  be  constituted  with  the  following

officials:

1) District Revenue Officer;
2) Superintendent of Police; and
3) Assistant Director of Survey.

(cid:153)  The District Level Review Committee shall meet at least once in a month
and communicate its finding, within 30 days from the date of receipt of such
petition, in a similar manner as described para 2 (iii).

(v)  District Level Steering Committee:

(cid:153)  The details and nature of complaints received by the 'Divisional Level Review
Committee'  and  'District  Level  Review  Committee'  along  with  the  gist  of
decisions taken, shall be presented before the existing 'District level Steering
Committee on Encroachment' headed by District Collector already constituted
vide G.O.Ms.No.105 Revenue Department dated 07.03.2001.

(cid:153)  A  written  communication  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  conveying  the
decision  of  the  above  Appellate  Committee  within  30  days  from  the
date of receipt of the Appeal Petition.

(iv)  Review Committee at District Level:

(cid:153)  Any  petitioner,  who  does  not  receive  reply  from  the  above  Appellate
Committee  within  the  stipulated  time  or  is  not  satisfied  with  the  decision  of
the  Appellate  Committee,  may  file  Review  Petition  before  the  Review
Committee.

(cid:153)  The  District  Level  Review  Committee  shall  be  constituted  with  the  following

officials:

1) District Revenue Officer;
2) Superintendent of Police; and
3) Assistant Director of Survey.

(cid:153)  The District Level Review Committee shall meet at least once in a month
and communicate its finding, within 30 days from the date of receipt of such
petition, in a similar manner as described para 2 (iii).

(v)  District Level Steering Committee:

(cid:153)  The details and nature of complaints received by the 'Divisional Level Review
Committee'  and  'District  Level  Review  Committee'  along  with  the  gist  of
decisions taken, shall be presented before the existing 'District level Steering
Committee on Encroachment' headed by District Collector already constituted
vide G.O.Ms.No.105 Revenue Department dated 07.03.2001.

context_str:
Source #1:
Act: Rev E 540 2014
Section: 3 - Orders of High Court of Madras in W.P.No.26722/2013, dated 08.10.2014.
Text: 3.  Orders of High Court of Madras in W.P.No.26722/2013, dated 08.10.2014.

ORDER:

----

In  reference  first  read  above,  the  Hon'ble  High  Court,  in  its  order  dated
11.08.2014 in W.P.No.26722/13 & M.P.No.1/13 filed by Thiru U.A. Marappa Gounder,
has ordered as follows:-

The State Government must set forth a mechanism where aggrieved
persons  can  complain  and  remedial  action  can  be  taken.    Promptly,  they
are  called  upon  to  do  so.    We  have  thus,  requested  the  learned
Government  Pleader  to  enter  appearance  in  this  matter  and  to  obtain
instructions from the State Government, in what manner such an authority
be set up, due  publicity  given to  the  authorities where aggrieved  persons
would  be  first  required  to  make  their  representations  and  a  remedial
action and reasoned response be communicated within a period of 30 days
from such complaint being made.  Once such a mechanism is set up, there
would  be  no  occasion  to  entertain  a  PIL,  on  the  first  instance  in  such
matters as an administrative machinery would be available for remedying
the position.

2.

Based  on  the  directions  of  the  orders  of  the  Hon'ble  High  Court  of
Madras, dated 08.10.2014, the Government have carefully examined the proposal of
the Additional Chief Secretary and Commissioner of Land Administration in reference

//p.t.o.//

 2

second  read  above  and  hereby,  accord  sanction  for  constitution  of  Redressal
Committees for eviction of encroachment with the following procedures:-

(i)

General Procedure: In general, as per Section 7 of Tamil Nadu Land
Encroachment  Act,  1905,  before  taking  proceedings,  the  Collector  or
Tahsildar shall cause to serve on the person a notice calling upon him
to  show  cause  why  he  should  not  be  evicted.    Further  as  per  notice
under Section 6 of the Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905, any
person unauthorizedly occupying any land may be summarily evicted by
the  Collector,  Tahsildar,  Deputy  Tahsildar  (or  any  other  officers
authorized  by  the  State  Government  in  this  regard)  by  serving  such
notices  in  the  manner  prescribed  under  Section  25  of  Tamil  Nadu
Revenue Recovery Act, 1864.

(ii)  Original Petition to be filed at Taluk level:

(cid:153)  The  petitioners  seeking  grievances  for  eviction  of  encroachment  on
Government land may file original petition before the respective Tahsildars.

(cid:153)  On receipt of such petition, the Tahsildar may cause inspection at least at
the  level  of  Deputy  Tahsildar,  to  decide  whether  the  Government  land  is
being  encroached  or  not.    If  convinced  of  the  fact  that  the  Government
land  is  being  encroached,  he  shall  serve  a  "Show  Cause  Notice"  to  the
encroacher under Section 7 of Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905.
Subsequently,  other  procedure  as  laid  down  under  the  Tamil  Nadu  Land
Encroachment Act should be followed within a stipulated time.

(cid:153)  A  detailed  written  reply  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  giving  details  of
the action, if any, taken as per Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905
and the entire process shall be completed within 60 days from the date of
receipt of petition.

(iii)  Appellate Committee at Divisional level:

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  admit  only  the  Appeal  Petitions  on  eviction  of
encroachment  either  on  the  ground  of  failure  on  the  part  of  Tahsildar  to
give reply within 60 days or if the petitioner is not satisfied with the reply
given or action taken by the Tahsildar.

(cid:153)  The Appellate Committee shall be constituted with the following officials:

 1) Revenue Divisional Officer;
 2) Deputy Superintendent of Police; and
 3) Deputy Inspector of Survey.

 3

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  meet  atleast  once  in  a  month  and  decide  each  and
every case individually after giving an opportunity for the petitioner to be
heard.  The Committee shall also take up personal inspection, if required,
along with Tahsildar and Surveyors, after giving advance intimation to the
petitioner.

(cid:153)  A  written  communication  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  conveying  the
decision  of  the  above  Appellate  Committee  within  30  days  from  the
date of receipt of the Appeal Petition.

(iv)  Review Committee at District Level:

(cid:153)  Any  petitioner,  who  does  not  receive  reply  from  the  above  Appellate
Committee  within  the  stipulated  time  or  is  not  satisfied  with  the  decision  of
the  Appellate  Committee,  may  file  Review  Petition  before  the  Review
Committee.

(cid:153)  The  District  Level  Review  Committee  shall  be  constituted  with  the  following

officials:

1) District Revenue Officer;
2) Superintendent of Police; and
3) Assistant Director of Survey.

(cid:153)  The District Level Review Committee shall meet at least once in a month
and communicate its finding, within 30 days from the date of receipt of such
petition, in a similar manner as described para 2 (iii).

(v)  District Level Steering Committee:

(cid:153)  The details and nature of complaints received by the 'Divisional Level Review
Committee'  and  'District  Level  Review  Committee'  along  with  the  gist  of
decisions taken, shall be presented before the existing 'District level Steering
Committee on Encroachment' headed by District Collector already constituted
vide G.O.Ms.No.105 Revenue Department dated 07.03.2001.

Source #1:
Act: Revenue E 234 2018
Section: 3 - In the letter second read above, the Principal Secretary/Commissioner of
Text: 3. In the letter second read above, the Principal Secretary/Commissioner of
Revenue  Administration  has  stated  that  the  Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster
Management Authority and Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management Agency have
the  same  acronym  viz.,  TNSDMA,  which  is  used  interchangeably,  causing  much
confusion  even  in  the  usual  course  of  daily  administration.    For  instance,  the
office of the  Accountant General during its performance Audit on Chennai Floods
2015, on many occasions misunderstood “Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management

/p.t.o/

 2

Agency”  as  “Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster  Management  Authority”  due  to  the
acronym  of  Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster  Management  Agency.    Hence,  for  clarity,
he has suggested that the Tamil Nadu State Disaster Management Agency which
is functioning  under the supervision of Commissioner of Revenue Administration,
Disaster  Management  and  Mitigation  Department  may  be      renamed  as  Tamil
Nadu Disaster Risk Reduction Agency, with an acronym of TNDRRA and therefore
requested  orders  of  Government  to  rename  Tamil  Nadu  State  Disaster
Management  Agency  (TNSDMA)  as  Tamil  Nadu  Disaster  Risk  Reduction
Agency (TNDRRA).

reservation under rule 13 of the Tamil Nadu Land Reforms (Disposal of Surplus Land)

 3

Rules,  1965  for  ELCOT,  Indian  Institute  of  Technology  and  Neyveli  Lignite
Corporation is cancelled.

(b) Reserving the lands to an extent of 88.52 acres in Kilambakkam Village in
favour of Chennai Metropolitan Development Authority for formation of new Mofussil
Bus  Terminus  at  Vandalur  (Kilambakkam  Village)  under  rule  13  of  the  Tamil  Nadu
Land  Reforms  (Disposal  of  Surplus  Land)  Rules,  1965  as  per  the  Announcement
made by the Hon’ble Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu under rule 110.

(c) As per the orders of the Hon'ble High Court, decision will be taken on the

representation of M/s.Kasturi Estate Private Limited, if submitted.

(d)    Regarding  the  land  value  to  be  paid  by  the  Chennai  Metropolitan

Development Authority, separate orders will be issued.

(e)  The  District  Collector,  Kancheepuram  is  also  directed  to  hand  over  the

lands to the Chennai Metropolitan Development Authority as per this order.

(BY ORDER OF THE GOVERNOR)

Administration  has  also  stated  that  in  the  Government  Order  third  read
above  annual  family  income  is  fixed  as  Rs.24,000/-  to  get  relief  under
Distress Relief Scheme. Though the income limit fixed as Rs.24,000/- to
get  relief  under  Distress  Relief  Scheme  exists,  the  same  analogy  is  being
followed  to  get  relief  under  Chief  Minister's  Public  Relief  Fund  also.    Till
date,  this  income  limit  is  in  force  for  more  than  5  years  without  any
revision.  He  has  also  suggested  that  in  view  of  the  price  hike  in  essential
things  at  present,  the  annual  family  income  of  victim  for  eligibility  under
Chief  Minister's  Public  Relief  Fund    may  be  revised  from  Rs.24,000/-  to
Rs.48,000/-

context_str:
Source #1:
Act: Rev E 540 2014
Section: 3 - Orders of High Court of Madras in W.P.No.26722/2013, dated 08.10.2014.
Text: 3.  Orders of High Court of Madras in W.P.No.26722/2013, dated 08.10.2014.

ORDER:

----

In  reference  first  read  above,  the  Hon'ble  High  Court,  in  its  order  dated
11.08.2014 in W.P.No.26722/13 & M.P.No.1/13 filed by Thiru U.A. Marappa Gounder,
has ordered as follows:-

The State Government must set forth a mechanism where aggrieved
persons  can  complain  and  remedial  action  can  be  taken.    Promptly,  they
are  called  upon  to  do  so.    We  have  thus,  requested  the  learned
Government  Pleader  to  enter  appearance  in  this  matter  and  to  obtain
instructions from the State Government, in what manner such an authority
be set up, due  publicity  given to  the  authorities where aggrieved  persons
would  be  first  required  to  make  their  representations  and  a  remedial
action and reasoned response be communicated within a period of 30 days
from such complaint being made.  Once such a mechanism is set up, there
would  be  no  occasion  to  entertain  a  PIL,  on  the  first  instance  in  such
matters as an administrative machinery would be available for remedying
the position.

2.

Based  on  the  directions  of  the  orders  of  the  Hon'ble  High  Court  of
Madras, dated 08.10.2014, the Government have carefully examined the proposal of
the Additional Chief Secretary and Commissioner of Land Administration in reference

//p.t.o.//

 2

second  read  above  and  hereby,  accord  sanction  for  constitution  of  Redressal
Committees for eviction of encroachment with the following procedures:-

(i)

General Procedure: In general, as per Section 7 of Tamil Nadu Land
Encroachment  Act,  1905,  before  taking  proceedings,  the  Collector  or
Tahsildar shall cause to serve on the person a notice calling upon him
to  show  cause  why  he  should  not  be  evicted.    Further  as  per  notice
under Section 6 of the Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905, any
person unauthorizedly occupying any land may be summarily evicted by
the  Collector,  Tahsildar,  Deputy  Tahsildar  (or  any  other  officers
authorized  by  the  State  Government  in  this  regard)  by  serving  such
notices  in  the  manner  prescribed  under  Section  25  of  Tamil  Nadu
Revenue Recovery Act, 1864.

(ii)  Original Petition to be filed at Taluk level:

(cid:153)  The  petitioners  seeking  grievances  for  eviction  of  encroachment  on
Government land may file original petition before the respective Tahsildars.

(cid:153)  On receipt of such petition, the Tahsildar may cause inspection at least at
the  level  of  Deputy  Tahsildar,  to  decide  whether  the  Government  land  is
being  encroached  or  not.    If  convinced  of  the  fact  that  the  Government
land  is  being  encroached,  he  shall  serve  a  "Show  Cause  Notice"  to  the
encroacher under Section 7 of Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905.
Subsequently,  other  procedure  as  laid  down  under  the  Tamil  Nadu  Land
Encroachment Act should be followed within a stipulated time.

(cid:153)  A  detailed  written  reply  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  giving  details  of
the action, if any, taken as per Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905
and the entire process shall be completed within 60 days from the date of
receipt of petition.

(iii)  Appellate Committee at Divisional level:

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  admit  only  the  Appeal  Petitions  on  eviction  of
encroachment  either  on  the  ground  of  failure  on  the  part  of  Tahsildar  to
give reply within 60 days or if the petitioner is not satisfied with the reply
given or action taken by the Tahsildar.

(cid:153)  The Appellate Committee shall be constituted with the following officials:

 1) Revenue Divisional Officer;
 2) Deputy Superintendent of Police; and
 3) Deputy Inspector of Survey.

 3

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  meet  atleast  once  in  a  month  and  decide  each  and
every case individually after giving an opportunity for the petitioner to be
heard.  The Committee shall also take up personal inspection, if required,
along with Tahsildar and Surveyors, after giving advance intimation to the
petitioner.

(cid:153)  A  written  communication  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  conveying  the
decision  of  the  above  Appellate  Committee  within  30  days  from  the
date of receipt of the Appeal Petition.

(iv)  Review Committee at District Level:

(cid:153)  Any  petitioner,  who  does  not  receive  reply  from  the  above  Appellate
Committee  within  the  stipulated  time  or  is  not  satisfied  with  the  decision  of
the  Appellate  Committee,  may  file  Review  Petition  before  the  Review
Committee.

(cid:153)  The  District  Level  Review  Committee  shall  be  constituted  with  the  following

officials:

1) District Revenue Officer;
2) Superintendent of Police; and
3) Assistant Director of Survey.

(cid:153)  The District Level Review Committee shall meet at least once in a month
and communicate its finding, within 30 days from the date of receipt of such
petition, in a similar manner as described para 2 (iii).

(v)  District Level Steering Committee:

(cid:153)  The details and nature of complaints received by the 'Divisional Level Review
Committee'  and  'District  Level  Review  Committee'  along  with  the  gist  of
decisions taken, shall be presented before the existing 'District level Steering
Committee on Encroachment' headed by District Collector already constituted
vide G.O.Ms.No.105 Revenue Department dated 07.03.2001.

land  is  being  encroached,  he  shall  serve  a  "Show  Cause  Notice"  to  the
encroacher under Section 7 of Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905.
Subsequently,  other  procedure  as  laid  down  under  the  Tamil  Nadu  Land
Encroachment Act should be followed within a stipulated time.

(cid:153)  A  detailed  written  reply  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  giving  details  of
the action, if any, taken as per Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905
and the entire process shall be completed within 60 days from the date of
receipt of petition.

(iii)  Appellate Committee at Divisional level:

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  admit  only  the  Appeal  Petitions  on  eviction  of
encroachment  either  on  the  ground  of  failure  on  the  part  of  Tahsildar  to
give reply within 60 days or if the petitioner is not satisfied with the reply
given or action taken by the Tahsildar.

(cid:153)  The Appellate Committee shall be constituted with the following officials:

 1) Revenue Divisional Officer;
 2) Deputy Superintendent of Police; and
 3) Deputy Inspector of Survey.

 3

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  meet  atleast  once  in  a  month  and  decide  each  and
every case individually after giving an opportunity for the petitioner to be
heard.  The Committee shall also take up personal inspection, if required,
along with Tahsildar and Surveyors, after giving advance intimation to the
petitioner.

(cid:153)  A  written  communication  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  conveying  the
decision  of  the  above  Appellate  Committee  within  30  days  from  the
date of receipt of the Appeal Petition.

(iv)  Review Committee at District Level:

(cid:153)  Any  petitioner,  who  does  not  receive  reply  from  the  above  Appellate
Committee  within  the  stipulated  time  or  is  not  satisfied  with  the  decision  of
the  Appellate  Committee,  may  file  Review  Petition  before  the  Review
Committee.

(cid:153)  The  District  Level  Review  Committee  shall  be  constituted  with  the  following

officials:

1) District Revenue Officer;
2) Superintendent of Police; and
3) Assistant Director of Survey.

(cid:153)  The District Level Review Committee shall meet at least once in a month
and communicate its finding, within 30 days from the date of receipt of such
petition, in a similar manner as described para 2 (iii).

(v)  District Level Steering Committee:

(cid:153)  The details and nature of complaints received by the 'Divisional Level Review
Committee'  and  'District  Level  Review  Committee'  along  with  the  gist  of
decisions taken, shall be presented before the existing 'District level Steering
Committee on Encroachment' headed by District Collector already constituted
vide G.O.Ms.No.105 Revenue Department dated 07.03.2001.

context_str:
Source #1:
Act: Rev E 540 2014
Section: 3 - Orders of High Court of Madras in W.P.No.26722/2013, dated 08.10.2014.
Text: 3.  Orders of High Court of Madras in W.P.No.26722/2013, dated 08.10.2014.

ORDER:

----

In  reference  first  read  above,  the  Hon'ble  High  Court,  in  its  order  dated
11.08.2014 in W.P.No.26722/13 & M.P.No.1/13 filed by Thiru U.A. Marappa Gounder,
has ordered as follows:-

The State Government must set forth a mechanism where aggrieved
persons  can  complain  and  remedial  action  can  be  taken.    Promptly,  they
are  called  upon  to  do  so.    We  have  thus,  requested  the  learned
Government  Pleader  to  enter  appearance  in  this  matter  and  to  obtain
instructions from the State Government, in what manner such an authority
be set up, due  publicity  given to  the  authorities where aggrieved  persons
would  be  first  required  to  make  their  representations  and  a  remedial
action and reasoned response be communicated within a period of 30 days
from such complaint being made.  Once such a mechanism is set up, there
would  be  no  occasion  to  entertain  a  PIL,  on  the  first  instance  in  such
matters as an administrative machinery would be available for remedying
the position.

2.

Based  on  the  directions  of  the  orders  of  the  Hon'ble  High  Court  of
Madras, dated 08.10.2014, the Government have carefully examined the proposal of
the Additional Chief Secretary and Commissioner of Land Administration in reference

//p.t.o.//

 2

second  read  above  and  hereby,  accord  sanction  for  constitution  of  Redressal
Committees for eviction of encroachment with the following procedures:-

(i)

General Procedure: In general, as per Section 7 of Tamil Nadu Land
Encroachment  Act,  1905,  before  taking  proceedings,  the  Collector  or
Tahsildar shall cause to serve on the person a notice calling upon him
to  show  cause  why  he  should  not  be  evicted.    Further  as  per  notice
under Section 6 of the Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905, any
person unauthorizedly occupying any land may be summarily evicted by
the  Collector,  Tahsildar,  Deputy  Tahsildar  (or  any  other  officers
authorized  by  the  State  Government  in  this  regard)  by  serving  such
notices  in  the  manner  prescribed  under  Section  25  of  Tamil  Nadu
Revenue Recovery Act, 1864.

(ii)  Original Petition to be filed at Taluk level:

(cid:153)  The  petitioners  seeking  grievances  for  eviction  of  encroachment  on
Government land may file original petition before the respective Tahsildars.

(cid:153)  On receipt of such petition, the Tahsildar may cause inspection at least at
the  level  of  Deputy  Tahsildar,  to  decide  whether  the  Government  land  is
being  encroached  or  not.    If  convinced  of  the  fact  that  the  Government
land  is  being  encroached,  he  shall  serve  a  "Show  Cause  Notice"  to  the
encroacher under Section 7 of Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905.
Subsequently,  other  procedure  as  laid  down  under  the  Tamil  Nadu  Land
Encroachment Act should be followed within a stipulated time.

(cid:153)  A  detailed  written  reply  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  giving  details  of
the action, if any, taken as per Tamil Nadu Land Encroachment Act, 1905
and the entire process shall be completed within 60 days from the date of
receipt of petition.

(iii)  Appellate Committee at Divisional level:

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  admit  only  the  Appeal  Petitions  on  eviction  of
encroachment  either  on  the  ground  of  failure  on  the  part  of  Tahsildar  to
give reply within 60 days or if the petitioner is not satisfied with the reply
given or action taken by the Tahsildar.

(cid:153)  The Appellate Committee shall be constituted with the following officials:

 1) Revenue Divisional Officer;
 2) Deputy Superintendent of Police; and
 3) Deputy Inspector of Survey.

 3

(cid:153)  This  Committee  shall  meet  atleast  once  in  a  month  and  decide  each  and
every case individually after giving an opportunity for the petitioner to be
heard.  The Committee shall also take up personal inspection, if required,
along with Tahsildar and Surveyors, after giving advance intimation to the
petitioner.

(cid:153)  A  written  communication  should  be  sent  to  the  petitioner  conveying  the
decision  of  the  above  Appellate  Committee  within  30  days  from  the
date of receipt of the Appeal Petition.

(iv)  Review Committee at District Level:

(cid:153)  Any  petitioner,  who  does  not  receive  reply  from  the  above  Appellate
Committee  within  the  stipulated  time  or  is  not  satisfied  with  the  decision  of
the  Appellate  Committee,  may  file  Review  Petition  before  the  Review
Committee.

(cid:153)  The  District  Level  Review  Committee  shall  be  constituted  with  the  following

officials:

1) District Revenue Officer;
2) Superintendent of Police; and
3) Assistant Director of Survey.

(cid:153)  The District Level Review Committee shall meet at least once in a month
and communicate its finding, within 30 days from the date of receipt of such
petition, in a similar manner as described para 2 (iii).

(v)  District Level Steering Committee:

(cid:153)  The details and nature of complaints received by the 'Divisional Level Review
Committee'  and  'District  Level  Review  Committee'  along  with  the  gist  of
decisions taken, shall be presented before the existing 'District level Steering
Committee on Encroachment' headed by District Collector already constituted
vide G.O.Ms.No.105 Revenue Department dated 07.03.2001.

Appeal  in  W.A.No.1519  of  2015  filed  by  the  State  and  set  aside  the  order  of  the
Learned  Single  Judge  dated  8.7.2015  and  in  respect  of  W.A.No.1289  and  1495  of
2015, filed by Tmt. Karpagam, it disposed of with the observation that the priority of
the Government is only to keep in mind the avowed object of allotment of land to the
needy  for  the  purpose  of  earning  livelihood  and  not  to  make  a  person  unjustly
enriched by way of such allotment and it is for the Government to provide suitable
alternate place to Tmt. Karpagam for the purpose of earning livelihood on 30.8.2016.
